title: Title IV: the US federal financial-aid lifecycle
summary: Federal student aid is a regulated pipeline from FAFSA to disbursement — and back, if a student withdraws. Today: the SAI that replaced the EFC, how need is calculated, the 2026-27 Pell maximum, and the Return of Title IV (R2T4) rule.

activities.title: How federal aid is calculated
activities.markdown:
A US student files the **FAFSA**; the school then computes need:

> **Need = Cost of Attendance (COA) − Student Aid Index (SAI) − other financial assistance**

The big recent change: the **FAFSA Simplification Act replaced the Expected Family Contribution (EFC) with the Student Aid Index (SAI)** beginning in the **2024-25** award year. Unlike EFC, the **SAI can be negative — as low as −1,500** — to better signal high need.

The **maximum Federal Pell Grant is $7,395 for 2026-27** (July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027).

sources.title: How Financial Aid Is Calculated — Federal Student Aid

activities.question: The FAFSA Simplification Act replaced the Expected Family Contribution (EFC) with what, starting in 2024-25?
activities.explanation: Beginning with the 2024-25 award year, the SAI replaced the EFC as the index used to determine federal aid eligibility.

activities.question: Unlike the old EFC, the Student Aid Index (SAI) can be…
options: negative — as low as −1,500 (signaling higher need)
activities.explanation: The SAI can go as low as −1,500. A negative SAI indicates greater financial need than EFC's floor of zero could express.

activities.question: A Title IV recipient withdraws after completing more than 60% of the payment period. Under R2T4, what happens?
options: 100% of the aid is considered earned — no return required
100% of the aid must be returned
activities.explanation: Return of Title IV (R2T4) prorates earned aid by time enrolled — but once a student passes 60% of the payment period, 100% of the aid is treated as earned and no return is required.

sources.title: Return of Title IV Funds — FSA

activities.title: The Title IV aid lifecycle
activities.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[Student submits FAFSA] --> B[School receives ISIR / SAI]
  B --> C[Verification if selected]
  C --> D[Package and award: COA - SAI - aid]
  D --> E[Disburse aid]
  E --> F[R2T4 recalculation if student withdraws]

steps.title: Submit FAFSA
steps.caption: The student applies for federal aid.

steps.title: ISIR / SAI
steps.caption: The school receives the processed FAFSA (ISIR) with the SAI.

steps.title: Verification
steps.caption: If selected, the school confirms the reported data.

steps.title: Package & award
steps.caption: Need = COA − SAI − other aid; the school builds the award package.

steps.title: Disburse
steps.caption: Aid is disbursed to the student's account.

steps.title: R2T4 on withdrawal
steps.caption: If the student withdraws, recalculate earned vs unearned aid.

activities.caption: This pipeline maps cleanly onto SIS states + events — and the R2T4 branch is the one most likely to be done wrong.

sources.title: How Financial Aid Is Calculated — FSA
Return of Title IV Funds — FSA

activities.headline: 2026-27 maximum Pell Grant set at $7,395
activities.summary: For the **2026-27 award year** (July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027), the maximum **Federal Pell Grant** is **$7,395**. The exact amount a student receives depends on their SAI, cost of attendance, enrollment intensity, and whether they attend a full year.

source.title: Federal Pell Grants — Federal Student Aid

items: **Need = COA − SAI − other aid**; the **SAI replaced the EFC** in 2024-25 and can be **negative (to −1,500)**.
**ISIR** = the processed FAFSA record the school receives.
**R2T4**: past **60%** of the payment period, all aid is earned — model withdrawal as a recalculation event.

glossary.definition: Index from the FAFSA used to determine federal aid eligibility (replaced the EFC in 2024-25); can be negative, as low as −1,500.
Total estimated annual cost to attend (tuition, housing, books, etc.); the starting point for need.
Institutional Student Information Record — the processed FAFSA data (including federal tax info) sent to schools.
Return of Title IV Funds — recalculation of earned aid when a recipient withdraws; past 60% of the term, all aid is earned.

sources.title: Return of Title IV Funds — FSA

reflection: Model the aid record in ManageArk as a state machine: SAI received → packaged → disbursed → (withdrawn → R2T4). What events trigger an R2T4 recalculation, and who must approve a return?
